meta.value: 6 plan components · 8 new fields · 9 decision sites · 3 sub-phases

blocks.heading: Give every AI GM a multi-year strategic plan — and make GM skill show up as how accurately they see it.

rows.value: A layered **StrategicPlan** (window classification + 5 boards) that sits *above* the GM's existing reactive, per-event decisions.
Today's GM brain only reacts — a contract expires → evaluate; an offer arrives → evaluate. There is no multi-year roster/cap board, no target lists, no draft/dev feedback loop.
The three still-weak `gm_capManagement` correlations — *structurally*, not by bolting on more per-decision noise.
856-line design · 6 plan components · 8 new GM/owner fields · touches 9 decision sites · ships in 3 sub-phases.

bigIdea.label: The big idea
bigIdea.sub: Every GM builds an equally detailed plan. **Bad GMs mis-perceive** cap projections, mis-rank targets, misclassify their contention window, and miss cliff years. **Good GMs see the world accurately.** GM skill becomes *plan-perception quality* — the structural mechanism that closes the weak correlations, instead of one more noise dial.

blocks.intro: The strategic plan is a new top layer. World state is filtered through skill-noised perception into the plan; the plan feeds GM, coach, and owner decisions and derives intent/budget for the existing `CompliancePlan`. The value model and negotiation engine below stay untouched.
blocks.title: Information flow
The plan: 6 components
blocks.intro: The plan is a window classifier plus five boards. The signature move: **every board splits into a factual core and a perceived layer** — this is where GM skill lives.

left.title: Factual core
left.desc: Computed deterministically from world state. **Identical for every GM.** The arithmetic. Validation checks it against world state directly.

right.title: Perceived layer
right.desc: GM-skill-noised judgment + projection. **Where skill differentiates.** Validation checks it against expected noise shapes.

anatomy.caption: Same world, two GMs, two plans: a 90-skill GM's perceived layer hugs the truth; a 40-skill GM's drifts — mis-ranked targets, missed cliffs, an over-confident window read.

cards.name: WindowClassification
cards.purpose: Are we a contender, bubble, retooler, or rebuilder — and how sure?

cards.name: CapBoard
cards.purpose: 5-year cap outlook: ceiling, commitments, dead cap — and which years are cliffs.

cards.name: OrgDepthChart
cards.purpose: Who fills each position now and projected, sorted into core / bridge / depth tiers.

cards.name: ProScoutingBoard
cards.purpose: Ranked FA + trade-deadline targets (Plan A/B/C) per need slot, with reservation prices.

cards.name: DraftBoard
cards.purpose: Prospect rankings, need-adjusted by position — the GM's view through their scouting org.

cards.name: PipelineMap
cards.purpose: When each prospect is NHL-ready, and pipeline health (rich / normal / thin) per position.

blocks.note: **Deputy modulation:** `scoutingNetwork` adds to effective skill on scouting-driven fields — a 50-skill GM with an elite (90) scouting network sees a ~70-skill view. Good GM hires good scouts → compounding.

blocks.title: Where it fits
blocks.intro: 2m owns *which positions/players and when*. It slots between the model that prices a budget and the engine that lands a number.

chain.title: Cap-spend value model
chain.desc: decides *how* a given budget is executed

chain.title: Negotiation realism
chain.desc: decides *what number* gets signed — reads via `evaluateExtensionCandidate`

stack.label: StrategicPlan
stack.note: the new top layer — window + 5 boards

stack.label: CompliancePlan
stack.note: consumes derived intent + signingBudget; keeps its lever-cost machinery

blocks.title: Key decisions
blocks.intro: Locked in the 2026-05-31 brainstorm — the load-bearing choices the rest of the design rests on.

decisions.why: makes skill *structural* — the mechanism to close the weak correlations, not one more per-decision noise dial.
decisions.rejected: skill scaling plan *detail* (weak GMs build thinner plans) — real weak GMs plan in full, they're wrong not lazy, and detail-scaling resists validation.

decisions.why: separable concerns — each board carries its own update cadence and consumers.

decisions.a: GM owns all; deputy stats modulate quality
decisions.why: avoids building deputy *agents* (deferred); reuses the existing `scoutingNetwork` stat — good GM hires good scouts, compounding.

decisions.a: Medium layer — strategic informs intent/budget/targets; compliance keeps levers
decisions.why: preserves the shipped, tuned lever-cost machinery instead of rewriting what already works.
decisions.rejected: full replacement (discards the tuned levers) and a thin annotation layer (wouldn't shift behavior) — only a medium layer moves intent/budget without regressing the cap work.

decisions.a: Soft prior — dominant default, breakable by personality / owner / opportunity
decisions.why: a dominant default keeps GM behavior realistic without making it rigid.
decisions.rejected: a hard gate where the window strictly dictates the option set — produces robotic GMs that can't pounce on a value opportunity or bend to owner pressure.

decisions.why: discrete states are legible and testable; confidence carries uncertainty without continuous-state complexity.

decisions.a: Partial via role-scoped views (coach + owner; no player/rival)
decisions.why: models real-NHL info silos — coach/owner consume slices; no player or rival leakage.

decisions.a: Each board: factual fields + perceived fields
decisions.why: lets validation check factual fields against world state and perceived fields against noise shapes — isolates where skill lives.
real GMs re-evaluate faster than the cadence anyway; carry-forward adds state + tuning for no clear benefit.

blocks.title: Scope
inList: Layered strategic plan — window classifier + 5 boards, each on its own cadence
Plan quality scales with `capManagement` / `talentEvaluation` / `riskAversion` / `planDiscipline` + deputy stats
Replace reactive `intent` + `signingBudget` derivation with plan-aware computation
Close the three weak `gm_capManagement` correlations structurally
Surface new emergent GM-quality signals (window mix, cliff avoidance, target walkdown, …)
Owner mandate becomes *structural* — a +25 score shift on window, modulated by `planDiscipline`
Disruption handlers as explicit per-event plan-delta functions

outList.text: Replacing the cap-spend value model — stays
Deputy *agents* with their own brains (GM owns all plans)
Cross-GM plan visibility, or a player view of the plan

blocks.title: Rollout
blocks.intro: Each phase has a hard exit gate — no advancing until the prior phase demonstrably holds the bands.

phases.title: Structural core
phases.scope: `capBoard`, `orgDepthChart`, `windowClassification` (sticky transitions + owner mandate), position budgets. Wires `intent` + `signingBudget` to plan-aware computation. Adds `coachView()` / `ownerView()`, new GM/Owner fields, and Owner-Brain mandate emission. **No target lists yet.**
gate: No regression on Phase 2d / value bands (util 90-100%, over/under = 0)

phases.title: Behavior layer
phases.scope: `proScoutingBoard` (FA + deadline target lists, Plan A/B/C + reservation prices), `pipelineMap` (full readiness), all disruption handlers. Wires into FA bidding, extensions, trade generation/evaluation, callup/sendDown. 2h's `evaluateExtensionCandidate` implemented against the real planner.
gate: Target-list walkdown fires (Plan A signs elsewhere → bid Plan B)
No Phase 2f arc-incidence or 2d cap-band regression

phases.title: Draft integration + calibration
phases.scope: `draftBoard` wired to draft-pick selection (plan-aware, need-adjusted ranking replaces the scout-blind-spot + `draftAcumen` heuristic). Full multi-seed calibration sweep. Spec-relaxation decision if correlations don't fully close.

blocks.title: Definition of done
blocks.intro: The whole point: move three correlations from ~zero to clearly negative — bad cap managers should visibly accrue dead cap, lose assets, and miss LTIR windows.

movers.name: gm_capManagement × dead_cap
movers.label: bad GMs stack cliff deals + stretch buyouts

movers.name: gm_capManagement × assets_lost
movers.label: bad GMs over-commit picks in misaligned trades

cells: bad GMs misperceive their own window
strong GMs structure to avoid cliffs; weak GMs stack them
Plan A lands sometimes, not always (real bidding wars)
each handler fires occasionally; total volume realistic
owner pressure exists but isn't dominant

blocks.note: Validation: 4 seeds × 25 seasons (2m.C); 4 × 15 (2m.A gate). In band if the median sits in tolerance for ≥3 of 4 seeds.

risks.risk: Cap-utilization regression in 2m.A as intent/budget derivation changes compliance behavior
risks.mitigation: 2m.A's hard exit gate *is* the existing band — no proceeding to 2m.B until it demonstrably holds.

risks.risk: Replan-cost explosion during burn-in (board rebuilds × 32 teams × 100 seasons)
risks.mitigation: dirty-flag per board; rebuild only boards whose inputs changed; perception is `O(roster)`, not `O(world)`.

risks.risk: Window classification thrashes — sticky transitions don't stick
risks.mitigation: unit tests on transition logic; multi-seed steady-state check shows stability over 5+ seasons for stable rosters.

risks.risk: Owner-mandate dominance — window driven entirely by the owner
risks.mitigation: bounded +25 shift; high-discipline GMs resist; calibration metric `flip_rate < 8%`.

risks.risk: Disruption-handler conflicts — concurrent events produce inconsistent state
risks.mitigation: explicit precedence rules; deltas merged on a copy; rollback on conflict detection.
enumerate all dials upfront; conservative defaults; tune only those that fail validation.

risks.risk: Performance-budget breach (perception × 6 boards × ~12 replans per team-season)

blocks.title: Before you approve
blocks.intro: What sign-off actually commits to, the one place to look hardest, and what's still open.

commit.title: What you're approving
commit.body: A **staged rollout, not a big bang.** Sign-off greenlights **2m.A** (the structural plumbing) first — behind a hard exit gate that *is* the existing Phase 2d / value-model band. **2m.B** (target lists, disruptions) and **2m.C** (draft + correlations) don't proceed until the prior phase demonstrably holds. Each phase is independently revertible.

scrutinize.title: Scrutinize hardest
scrutinize.body: The **decision-site integration** — the spec's own “most regression-prone section.” It rewires **9 GM decision sites** (intent/budget derivation, targeted bid sizing, extension anchors, trade-target ID, draft, levers) but keeps every tuned arithmetic core untouched (`value.ts`, `levers.ts`, `selectBest23`, `enumerateReliefOptions`). Risk is real but bounded — caught by 2m.A's gate before 2m.B adds target lists. [See the decision-site map ›](#ref-sites)

open.title: Still open — non-blocking
open.note: Explicitly *plan-stage inputs, decided during planning — not blockers to approval:*
questions: How far to project unfilled depth slots (Y0 only → Y0–Y4)?
Same-precedence disruption collisions on one field.
2m.C spec-relaxation criteria if a correlation lands at −0.32.

blocks.intro: Full depth, collapsed by default. Everything above gets you oriented; open these when you need the exact shapes.

items.html:
interface StrategicPlan {
  franchise:        FranchiseId
  generatedSeason:  number
  generatedDay:     number

  window:           WindowClassification
  capBoard:         CapBoard
  orgDepthChart:    OrgDepthChart
  proScoutingBoard: ProScoutingBoard
  draftBoard:       DraftBoard
  pipelineMap:      PipelineMap

  // role-scoped reads — no playerView()/rivalGmView()
  coachView(): CoachPlanView
  ownerView(): OwnerPlanView
}
type WindowState = 'contender' | 'bubble' | 'retooler' | 'rebuilder'

interface WindowClassification {
  state:                  WindowState
  confidence:             number               // 0-100, perceived margin ×2
  enteredSeason:          number               // sticky transitions
  expectedYearsRemaining: number
  reasons:                WindowReasonTag[]
  perceivedScores:        Record<WindowState, number>  // GM-noised
}
type Year05 = Float32Array  // length-5: [Y0..Y4]

interface CapBoard {
  // Factual — read from world state, same for all GMs
  ceiling:          Year05
  committedCapHit:  Year05
  deadCap:          Year05
  ltirPoolUsed:     number
  // Perceived — GM judgment + projection
  perceivedCliffYears:       number[]
  perceivedFlexibilityScore: number
  perceivedCeilingGrowthY3to4: number
}
interface OrgDepthChart {
  // Factual
  byPosition:    Record<Position, DepthSlot[]>
  contractEnds:  Record<PlayerId, number>
  // Perceived
  coreTier:      PlayerId[]   // 3-6 future/core players
  bridgeTier:    PlayerId[]
  depthTier:     PlayerId[]
  unfilledSlots: Record<NeedSlot, number[]>
}

interface ProScoutingBoard {
  faTargets:       Record<NeedSlot, TargetEntry[]>  // A/B/C per slot
  deadlineTargets: Record<NeedSlot, TargetEntry[]>
  watchList:       PlayerId[]
}
interface DraftBoard {
  ranked:          ProspectRanking[]
  needAdjusted:    Record<Position, ProspectRanking[]>
  finalRoundReady: boolean
  scoutingHorizon: number
}

interface PipelineMap {
  perProspect:           Record<PlayerId, ProspectReadiness>
  graduationCountByYear: Year05
  positionHealth:        Record<Position, PipelineHealth>
}

Every perceived field has a function of the same shape: perceiveX(truth, skill, deputy?, rng). Three noise flavors:

Continuous numeric

perceived = truth + N(0, σ),   σ = baseSigma × (100 − skill) / 100

Bounded to a valid range; directional bias (e.g. low capManagement → bullish on ceiling growth) adds a mean shift.

Categorical / bucketed

P(perceived_bucket ≠ truth_bucket) = (100 − skill) / k
P(shift 2 buckets)                  = much smaller (~5% mis-band)

Set membership (core players, cliff years)

per true qualifier:    P(included)         = f(skill)
per non-qualifier:     P(falsely included) = g(skill)

Determinism

Each call is seeded by hash(worldSeed, franchiseId, season, day, boardType, fieldKey) — same inputs, same perception. Re-rolling a board mid-season with unchanged world state yields the same perceived board (no “lucky day”). Deputy modulation: effectiveSkill = clamp(gmSkill + scoutingNetworkBoost, 0, 100).

Calendar skeleton (forced replans)

EventSim dayReplan scope
Season-endvariesFull — all 6 boards rebuilt
Pre-draft~315draftBoard locked; pipelineMap final pass
FA open (Jul 1)~335proScoutingBoard executes; capBoard rebuilt per bid
Mid-season (~game 41)~85window confidence; deadline targets seeded
Trade deadline130target lists locked; capBoard post-deadline rebuild

Threshold-triggered (ad-hoc)

Cap-affecting transaction → capBoard. Season-ending core injury → full window re-class. Prospect breakout → pipelineMap + window + depth slot. Target signs elsewhere → walk to Plan B + budget redistribution. Owner mandate change → full window. Cost discipline: boards rebuild only if their inputs changed (dirty flag per board).

items.summary: Decision-site map — what the plan replaces vs adds
items.html:
SiteReplacesKeeps
buildOffseasonPlanintent + signingBudget derivation; tradeablelever-cost machinery, cap-dump enumeration
generateGMOfferbid sizing for targeted players (uses reservationAAV)gmCapJudgmentBidMul, value perception for untargeted UFAs
Extensions (2h seam)offer-or-not gate; AAV anchor for core-tier2h negotiation arithmetic, comp-anchored valuation
generateTradeProposaltarget identification; position-sheddingtrade arithmetic, retention logic, relationship memory
evaluateTradeProposaladds a plan-fit axis alongside valuevalue perception, NTC/NMC gating
Draft selectionBPA-vs-need becomes plan-awarescout ingestion, blind spot, draftAcumen roll
Leverscost ranking becomes plan-aware (tier-sensitive)enumerateReliefOptions ranking (PR-B)

The recently-shipped tuning (value.ts, levers.ts, selectBest23, enumerateReliefOptions) stays untouched. What changes: which players enter the ranker, which intent runs, and what the budget total is.

interface GMProfile {  // + 6 new traits
  planDiscipline:         number   // mean 60 σ18 — Yzerman/Lamoriello high
  shinyObjectBias:        number   // inverse riskAversion + ego
  bridgeVsTermPreference: number
  ntcGenerosity:          number   // high-discipline GMs stingy
  ltirHeadroomPreference: number
  relationshipReading:    number   // reads NTC block probability
}

interface OwnerProfile {  // + mandate
  mandate?: 'compete' | 'rebuild' | 'unspecified'  // default unspecified
}

Franchise.scoutingNetwork already exists and modulates perception; a future capAnalystQuality for CapBoard is deferred.

A disruption is an event a handler converts to a PlanDelta applied to a copy of the plan. Kinds: star_injury, target_lost_in_fa, unexpected_offer, prospect_breakout, prospect_bust, rebuild_stall, coach_fired, key_player_trade_request, rival_blockbuster, owner_mandate_change.

Example — target_lost_in_fa: remove the lost target, promote Plan B; redistribute the unspent Plan A reservation (60% → Plan B, 40% → next slot’s Plan A). Example — prospect_breakout (Y2+ → Y0): bump readiness to now, shift graduation counts, upgrade position health, and if a rebuilder sees ≥2 breakouts, re-evaluate the window. Concurrent disruptions merge by precedence: owner-mandate > seasonality > severity-major > severity-minor.

Testing: unit tests per perception function (truth + skill → expected shape), per board rebuild, and per disruption handler; integration tests for the plan→decision pipeline parameterized over GM skill; a multi-seed calibration sweep; a snapshot test on a known seed (window distribution at season 10); and a full cross-system regression check (~2150 tests + Phase 2d/2f/2c gates).

Performance: sim target < 2ms/game. ~1000 replan events per season × <50µs/board × 6 boards = ~300µs/replan, distributing to <50µs per game from planning — achievable with pre-allocated buffers and seeded deterministic perception.
